q: Among the thinkers in this hub, who argues most directly against Stoic detachment, and on what grounds? Ground it in a corpus.
final:
I can’t answer this as asked from the hub, because the materials I retrieved don’t contain any explicit “argument against Stoic detachment” that I can ground in a corpus quote.

What I tried:
- A hub search for “against Stoic detachment thinker argues against detachment” returned a match for **Epictetus** (ru/hub/epictetus), but when I attempted to read that section, the content came back empty: `note_html(pid=1428)` returned `{"text": ""}` (https://trip2g.com/ru/hub/epictetus). Without readable text, I can’t extract grounds or quote anything.
- I also attempted a federated search into a corpus named `nietzsche`, but federation isn’t configured for that `kb_id` (“Federation is not configured for kb_id "nietzsche".”), so I couldn’t consult any other thinker texts that way.

If you tell me **which corpus/language** to use (e.g., “English hub”, or point to a specific thinker list in the hub), or if you re-run the query with more specific wording (e.g., “against apatheia”, “against ataraxia”, “against indifference”), I can search again and then read the exact matching sections so I can name the thinker and quote their stated grounds.
